title: The Ass in the Lion's Skin
lines: An Ass found the skin of a Lion lying in a cave.
He wrapped it around himself and walked out.
All the animals fled at his approach, thinking he was truly a Lion.
The Ass was delighted at his power and walked about in a very proud manner.
But presently he met a Fox, and the Fox stopped to look at him carefully.
"Are you really a Lion?" asked the Fox.
The Ass was so proud of his disguise that he opened his mouth to bray loudly.
The Fox immediately knew who he was.
"Ah," said the Fox, "I see now that you are nothing but an Ass in a Lion's skin."
moral: A fool may wear a wise man's clothes, but he cannot wear his wisdom.
